title: The One White Hair
content:
The wisest of the wise
Listen to pretty lies
And love to hear them told;
Doubt not that Solomon
Listenï؟½d to many a one,آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ 
Some in his youth, and more when he grew old.

I never was among
The choir of Wisdomï؟½s song,
But pretty lies lovï؟½d I
As much as any king,
When youth was on the wing,
And (must it then be told?) when youth had quite gone by.

Alas! and I have not
The pleasant hour forgot
When one pert lady said,
ï؟½O Walter! I am quite
Bewilderï؟½d with affright!
I see (sit quiet now) a white hair on your head!ï؟½

Another more benign
Snippï؟½d it away from mine,
And in her own dark hair
Pretended it was foundï؟½
She leapï؟½d, and twirlï؟½d it roundï؟½
Fair as she was, she never was so fair!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter Savage Landor delivers a powerful performance in "The One White Hair"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
